ud2.3:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud2.3:1.3:
And on that occasion,
a large number of boys on the road between Sāvatthī & Jeta’s Grove were hitting a snake with a stick.
ud2.3:1.4:
Then early in the morning the Blessed One adjusted his under robe and–
carrying his bowl & robes–went into Sāvatthī for alms.
ud2.3:1.5: He saw the large number of boys on the road between Sāvatthī & Jeta’s Grove hitting the snake with a stick.
ud2.3:2.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud2.3:3.3: when he himself is looking for ease,
ud2.3:3.4: will meet with no ease after death.
ud2.3:4.1: Whoever doesn’t hit with a stick
ud2.3:4.3: when he himself is looking for ease,
ud2.3:4.4: will meet with ease after death.
